questions.question: You are configuring a Wi-Fi base station using DHCP. Which items should you set or change?

choices.A:
A
- Reset administrator password.
- Set network name.
- Set network security.
- Set network password.
choices.B:
B
- Reset administrator password.
- Set network name.
- Set network password.
choices.C:
C
- Set network name.
- Set network password.
- Assign DNS servers.
choices.D:
D
- Change administrator account name.
- Set network password.
- Set network name.
- Assign DNS servers.

questions.question: Can you connect a DisplayPort monitor to a USB-C port on your computer?

choices.A: Yes. The DisplayPort cable plugs directly into the USB connector.
choices.B: Yes. An adapter or dongle may be required.
choices.C: No. While you can rig a connection, video quality will be poor.
choices.D: No. The two standards are incompatible.

choices.B: a cluster of ports and connectors found on the back of the computer case
choices.C: the switches that turn on, sleep, and turn off the computer
choices.D: the portion of the motherboard where non-power cables connect

questions.question: The computer consistently boots into Safe Mode. How would you address this issue?

choices.A: Edit the Registry key that controls the boot sequence.
choices.B: Hold the Shift key as Windows boots.
choices.C: Check the MSCONFIG utility to ensure that Normal boot is selected.
choices.D: Fix the issue in Safe Mode that is causing the system to restart in this mode.

choices.B:
B

C

2. Switch
choices.D: The order is not important.

choices.A: The connection between the display and the computer is loose.
choices.B: You need to reinstall the display drivers.
choices.C: You need to replace your monitor.
choices.D: Some of your RAM is failing.

choices.A: tracert
choices.B: netstat
choices.C: ping
choices.D: cURL

questions.question: Does an incognito window prevent websites from tracking your activity on the web?

choices.A: No. Incognito mode only prevents the site from appearing in your browser history.
choices.B: Yes. Incognito mode encrypts data packets, making it difficult for websites to track your activity.
choices.C: No. Some websites track by ID, and if you sign into a site even in incognito, your activity is known.
choices.D: Yes. Incognito mode blocks all cookies and tracking IDs and suppresses browser history.

questions.question: What must you confirm before performing a processor upgrade?

choices.A: The new processor is compatible with the current motherboard.
choices.B: The new processor is the same type as the old.
choices.C: The new processor is compatible with the operating system.
choices.D: The new processor fits into the old processor's slot.

questions.question: Which type of storage has the fastest data access speeds?

choices.A: Secure Sockets Layer drive
choices.B: solid-state drive
choices.C: serial attached storage device
choices.D: SCSI drive

questions.question: Which peripheral is most likely to require entering a Bluetooth pairing passcode?

choices.A: A. Keyboard
choices.B: B. Mouse
choices.C: C. Headset
choices.D:
D. Printer
> _Explanation:_ Many Bluetooth keyboards (especially older or “legacy” models) require entering a pairing/passcode during initial pairing. Mice and headsets can require pairing too, but keyboards historically + most commonly use a numeric pairing code. This question is somewhat hardware-dependent — see PR discussion for edge-cases and references.

---

questions.question: Walt's computer is several years old and he wants to ensure that the system works until he gets a replacement. What should he do first?

choices.A: Run a file check daily.
choices.B: Obtain an extended warranty.
choices.C: Back up his data.
choices.D:
Ensure that the system is protected against malware.
---

questions.question: Which statement best describes a drive?

choices.A: A. A drive is hardware that reads/writes data and often accepts removable media (e.g., disk, CD).
choices.B: B. A drive is a software component that manages files.
choices.C: C. A drive is a network service for file transfer.
choices.D:
D. A drive is an encryption method.
> _Explanation:_ A drive is a physical device that performs read/write operations; removable-media drives (CD/DVD, removable disk) accept media, while non-removable drives (SSD/HDD) provide storage without separate media.

---

questions.question: Mass storage inside a desktop computer case is usually found where?

choices.A: in an expansion slot
choices.B: attached to the front of the case
choices.C: in a drive cage
choices.D:
on the motherboard
---

questions.question: Which device is used to connect computers on a local network (LAN) at Layer 2?

choices.A: A. Switch (Layer 2)
choices.B: B. Server
choices.C: C. Router
choices.D:
D. Gateway
> _Explanation:_ A network switch operates at Layer 2 and forwards frames between devices on the same LAN. Servers provide services but do not serve the traffic-forwarding role of a switch.

---

questions.question: If you move a user’s data and installed programs from one system to another, this process is called:

choices.A: A. Migration
choices.B: B. Profile transfer
choices.C: C. Backup
choices.D:
D. Restore
> _Explanation:_ A migration moves data and installed applications/settings from one environment to another; a “profile transfer” generally refers only to user profile data.

---

questions.question: To access extra keys or alternate key functions on some laptop keyboards (compact layouts), which key is typically used?

choices.A: A. Alt/Option
choices.B: B. Ctrl
choices.C: C. Fn
choices.D:
D. Shift
> _Explanation:_ The **Fn** (Function) key is commonly used to access alternate functions on laptop/compact keyboards (media keys, secondary key legends). Alt/Option performs different roles and is not the usual way to access alternate hardware keysets.

---

questions.question: If a PC repeatedly boots into Safe Mode, what is the most appropriate next step?

choices.A: A. Reboot immediately and hope it resolves.
choices.B: B. Investigate the cause (Safe Mode indicates the system detected a problem).
choices.C: C. Reinstall the OS right away.
choices.D:
D. Ignore and continue using.
> _Explanation:_ Persistent boots into Safe Mode indicate underlying problems (driver, hardware, or config). Investigate logs, recent changes and safe-mode diagnostics before opting to reinstall or only restart. (A quick restart can be used as a test, but repeated Safe Mode boots should be diagnosed.)

questions.question: What is the technical reason why you must properly eject media?

choices.A: File I/O is buffered. If you remove the media before the buffer is cleared, files remain open and potentially corrupt.
choices.B: The Storage Inventory Manager (SIM) can lose its drive references, which may corrupt other storage in the system.
choices.C: The drive must receive the eject signal and turn itself off, dropping the DTR signal and indicating the media is safe to remove.
choices.D: The computer must stop the 5-volt signal to media, otherwise the drive may short.

choices.C: the device's NIC based on the MAC address

questions.question: In a hybrid cloud architecture, what is the main challenge of maintaining consistent configuration states across on-premises and cloud environments?

choices.A: Limited network throughput between environments
choices.B: Configuration drift due to asynchronous state management and differing IaC tool versions
choices.C: Incompatible authentication mechanisms between providers
choices.D: Lack of hardware abstraction at the virtualization layer

questions.question: In enterprise IT monitoring, what problem does "cardinality explosion" refer to?

choices.A: The exponential growth in metric series when combining multiple label dimensions (e.g., host, service, region)
choices.B: The overuse of alerting thresholds across distributed systems
choices.C: The inability to track metrics due to missing time-series indexes
choices.D: The merging of multiple metric stores into a single pipeline

questions.question: In a CI/CD pipeline, why is using **immutable infrastructure** considered best practice?

choices.A: It allows manual patching of existing servers to maintain consistency
choices.B: It ensures that each deployment creates a new environment instance, preventing configuration drift
choices.C: It eliminates the need for orchestration or automation tools
choices.D: It enables incremental updates instead of full rebuilds

questions.question: What issue arises when using synchronous microservice calls in a globally distributed production system?

choices.A: Increased CPU utilization in backend servers
choices.B: Reduced need for caching layers
choices.C: Simplified tracing and logging mechanisms
choices.D: High inter-service latency and cascading failures under network partitions

questions.question: In container orchestration systems like Kubernetes, what does the **control plane** primarily manage?

choices.A: The desired state of the cluster, ensuring workloads match declared configurations via reconciliation loops
choices.B: The real-time network routing between pods
choices.C: The physical resource allocation at the hardware level
choices.D: The filesystem synchronization across distributed nodes

questions.question: In distributed systems observability, what is the primary purpose of implementing distributed tracing with context propagation?

choices.A: To record only local system metrics
choices.B: To replicate log data across all services
choices.C: To track a single request across multiple services by passing unique identifiers through each span
choices.D: To store trace data directly within the message queue

questions.question: Why might an SRE team prefer a **Service Level Objective (SLO)** expressed as an error budget over a 100% uptime target?

choices.A: Because it balances reliability with development velocity and provides a measurable tolerance for controlled risk
choices.B: Because it enforces stricter deployment rules in CI/CD pipelines
choices.C: Because it automatically configures monitoring alerts based on uptime
choices.D: Because it eliminates the need for any downtime management

questions.question: What operational issue is most likely if a message broker in an event-driven system begins showing a consistent lag in queue depth?

choices.A: Excessive horizontal scaling of producers
choices.B: Consumers are processing messages slower than the production rate, causing backpressure
choices.C: Queue visibility timeout misconfiguration
choices.D: Duplicate message publishing from producers

questions.question: In large-scale infrastructure management, what’s a key operational advantage of **GitOps** over traditional configuration management approaches?

choices.A: It replaces container orchestration with simple shell automation
choices.B: It provides a declarative, version-controlled, and auditable single source of truth for infrastructure state
choices.C: It allows runtime state changes without version tracking
choices.D: It relies solely on imperative deployment scripts

questions.question: When autoscaling a stateful application, why can horizontal scaling lead to inconsistency issues?

choices.A: Because new replicas may lack synchronized persistent state or shared volume access, leading to data divergence
choices.B: Because CPU requests cannot be adjusted dynamically
choices.C: Because vertical scaling is disabled by default
choices.D: Because service discovery protocols fail to register new instances
